content.body: *Coming Soon!*
**Collected** brings calm to chaotic discoveries. Your favorite books, products, places, and experiences – once scattered across notes and apps – now live in one intuitive binder.
Collect privately, tag with mood and context, share what inspires. No image overload, no dopamine-chasing metrics. Just meaningful curation and genuine recommendations.

To evoke collecting’s truest symbol, Collected uses binder visual – the analog album that holds life’s treasures. Nostalgic page swipes deliver timeless tactile calm. Color-coded cards provide at-a-glance categorization.
**Context tags** add emotional depth – mood, discovery story, intention – turning finds into personal memories.
**Community space** allows you to share your best finds as well as get inpired by other’s favorites.

**Font becomes a muse.**
This quick type exploration, formed into an espresso bar brand is built on an exceptional open source font Frames Part One.

**Afterglow** is not a high-performance brand. It’s a low-lit espresso bar with dimmed lights, always offering refuge to every lost soul and wandering weirdo stumbling down the street.

**The logo** came to life by pairing Frames Part One with some funky hand-drawn illustrations, turning it into a customized symbol of wabi-sabi definition.

**High-contrast, exotic colors** tied the whole vision together – almost like the bar was assembled from old cargo crates straight off a harbor dock!

With **an identity** applied onto some brand mock-ups, this is the result of having a romantic dinner with Frames Part One, with some funky grooves playing in the background.

**Datova schranka** is a government messaging system used for sending official documents in file format (no plain text messages).
The goal of this redesign was to improve clarity, usability, and information hierarchy – while keeping the legal nature of the system intact.

The current Data Box system suffers from several usability issues:
overwhelming information density, poor visual hierarchy, a non-intuitive message composition flow, and attachments that are often difficult to access.

**The redesign therefore focuses on:**
making the interface feel familiar (email-like mental model); reducing visual noise and unnecessary UI elements; making attachments immediately accessible

**Familiar Mental Model (Email Inspiration)**
Since Datovka functions similarly to email (inbox, sent messages, attachments), the redesign builds on this familiar pattern.

**- Clear Inbox / Sent toggle
- Pull-to-refresh instead of a sync button
- Floating action button for composing messages
- Structured message list similar to mail apps

This significantly reduces the learning curve for users.**

**Another focus area was the message composition and detail flow.**
Since Datovka only allows file-based messages, users either upload an attachment or generate one from text. The redesign therefore prioritizes clear attachment handling and a simplified composition flow.

Inspired by familiar messaging patterns and supported by a simple design system, **the interface clearly separates app UI elements from official system data through typography and color.**
The result is a cleaner structure and more intuitive experience.
**Check out the app showcase with additional notes below!**
**PecDrive** is a street food restaurant with a built-in stone pizza oven right inside the truck — one of the things that makes it truly unique.

The goal of this project was to create **a visual identity** that reflects the hipster-like energy with which PecDrive crafts and serves its menu. **The logo** was meant to capture the main attributes of this street food concept — the car, the pizza, and the oven.

At first, combining these elements into a cohesive symbol was quite a challenge. But after experimenting with different concepts and a few rounds of brainstorming, this idea came to life — complemented by **playful illustrations** and **a bold, funky typeface** that resonates with the spirited character of PecDrive.

The whole identity is tied together with **a contrasting color palette** led by vibrant orange, symbolizing energy and appetite, inviting customers to enjoy those tasteful bites that PecDrive delivers from its truck.

After finalizing the brand identity, I designed **several print materials** including menus, billboards, and digital assets for social media presentation.

It was a real pleasure and a valuable experience to explore design within the gastronomic field — and I’m really happy with the results that came out of it. **Check out some insights into this project below!**

Take a browse through my curated collection of experimental posters exploring typography, composition, and visual rhythm.
An ongoing playground for ideas, intuition, and graphic experimentation.

**2D promotional animation created for a film festival.**
Inspired by the festival’s visual identity, the animation translates static design elements into motion — resulting in a short digital teaser concluding with the event date.
*Note: This is an unofficial concept project created as a personal initiative.*

projects.title: Digital & Print Identity for a Czech Cyclist

content.body:
**Antonie** is a young and talented cyclist from the Czech Republic, and this project was created to elevate her digital and personal brand.
It includes both a modern **website** and a **printed portfolio brochure** that together highlight her story, achievements, and personality.

**The website** focuses on key performance statistics, titles, and a dynamic blog section for recent updates, while the **printed portfolio** offers a more personal look — featuring **biometric data, career milestones, and design elements inspired by the Czech tricolor.
**The unified visual identity** ensures consistency across digital and print, strengthening her professional presentation to teams, sponsors, and fans.
